title: Fluffy Dutch Frankfurter Casserole
description: A kid-friendly Dutch dish featuring frankfurter, this casserole is jammy, aerated, and low-fat-friendly, making it a great option for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, cook the sliced frankfurters over medium heat until browned.
Add the chopped onion and sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until the vegetables are tender.
In a separate bowl, mix the tomato paste, low-fat milk, and beaten eggs.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, arrange a layer of cooked frankfurters and vegetables.
Pour the egg mixture over the frankfurter layer, then sprinkle with shredded cheese.
Repeat the layers until all ingredients are used, ending with a layer of cheese on top.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
